nativeRange.habitat: Rocky reef substrates and coralline rubble zones in the shallow photic zone; typically 0–30 m depth

avoidWith: sea urchins (may graze heavily on calcareous algae)

imageCandidates.notes: Product image from Mosaic Macros listing for Red Fern (Haliptilon sp.); shows fern-like red branching thallus

summary: Haliptilon sp. is an articulated calcareous red alga forming attractive fern-like red tufts in reef displays. Like sibling corallines Corallina officinalis and Jania adhaerens, it requires stable calcium and magnesium for its calcified jointed skeleton.
careNotes:
Red Fern is an articulated (geniculate) coralline alga in the family Corallinaceae — the same family as Coral Weed (Corallina officinalis, sw-algae-031) and Finefork Coralline (Jania adhaerens, sw-algae-037). It grows as upright, jointed, fern-like branching tufts described as resembling Caulerpa mexicana in branching form, with deep red coloration that has been noted to fluoresce under actinic lighting. Cuttings available from 1–2" plugs up to 5"+ fronds; the species reportedly reaches 12 cm in the wild.

As a calcareous alga, stable calcium and magnesium are essential for skeletal calcification. Reef parameters of Ca 400–440 ppm and Mg 1,250–1,400 ppm are generally advised for articulated corallines in this family. Alkalinity around 8–12 dKH supports carbonate availability. Elevated phosphate and nitrate have been reported to inhibit coralline calcification; maintaining phosphate < 0.05 ppm and nitrate < 5 ppm tends to support better performance.

Lighting should be moderate to high — high-intensity reef lighting is consistent with the requirements of articulated corallines in Corallinaceae. Growth rate is slow. Attach fragments to live rock or coral rubble; detached pieces that settle on rocky substrate can establish new colonies over time.

Mosaic Macros is the sole specialty aquarium trade source currently offering this species. Husbandry parameters are extrapolated from the well-documented articulated coralline care profile of its sibling genera within Corallinaceae (Corallina officinalis and Jania adhaerens), both of which share the same calcification chemistry and reef parameter requirements. Regular water changes help replenish calcium, carbonates, and trace elements for sustained growth. Per Mosaic care guide guidance: as a red or brown alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. (Sand-bed acceptance per Mosaic is limited to Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea genera, plus some calcified Dasycladales.)
breedingNotes: Propagation in captivity is primarily by fragmentation — detached thallus segments can attach to rock and establish new growth. Sexual reproduction via alternation of gametophyte and sporophyte generations also occurs but is not commonly observed in closed aquarium systems.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby trade source; confirmed species identity (Haliptilon sp.), growth form (fern-like red branching thallus, fluoresces under actinic), size range (1–2" cutting to 5"+ fronds). Product page lacks explicit water parameter or lighting specifications.

additional.name: Sibling species — Coral Weed (Corallina officinalis, sw-algae-031)
additional.notes: Corallina officinalis is a co-family articulated coralline (Corallinaceae, Corallinales). Water parameters, lighting, growth rate, placement, care level, and calcification requirements extrapolated from this sibling per playbook guidance for Mosaic-only articulated coralline entries.

additional.name: Sibling species — Finefork Coralline (Jania adhaerens, sw-algae-037)
additional.notes: Jania adhaerens is a second co-family articulated coralline (Corallinaceae, Corallinales). Cross-confirmed identical family-level care profile for geniculate corallines: high lighting, slow growth, calcareous form, display placement, low nutrient uptake, fragmentation propagation.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
